steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "did just that" is crucial. It means the fountain actually achieved Duchamp's intention to challenge prevailing conceptions about art.

steps.title: Match the confirmation logic
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation: Duchamp intended Fountain to challenge art conventions, and the second sentence confirms it actually did so. The blank needs a transition signaling that the following sentence affirms what was just stated.
  • "In addition,": signals additive information (a new, separate point), so fails — confirmation is not addition. ✗
  • "Similarly,": signals parallel comparison between two things, so fails — there is nothing being compared here. ✗
  • "Instead.": signals contrast or replacement, so fails — the second sentence does not contradict the first. ✗
  • "Indeed,": signals emphatic confirmation, fitting perfectly — it affirms that Duchamp's intention was realized. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
